writs.title: Astrolabe structured observations and draft-writ emission

decisions.question: What fields does the Observation record carry?
decisions.rationale: #18 — earn new structure from a second consumer; additional fields are speculation.

decisions.question: Should the `observations-write` tool param accept only the new Observation[] shape, or accept either the new shape or the legacy string?
decisions.rationale: #10 — no legacy in new stuff; Mk 2.1 doesn't bridge to the old shape.

decisions.question: Who generates `Observation.id` — the sage role writing the record, or the tool / engine?
decisions.rationale: #3 — extend the existing sibling pattern (sage already emits ids on ScopeItem/Decision arrays).

decisions.question: Should the observations-write tool validate that ids are unique within the array, and that title/body are non-empty?
decisions.rationale: #3 — extend the existing tool-layer contract (decisions-write/scope-write) rather than invent a parallel validation layer.

decisions.question: How does the astrolabe detail page render the new Observation[] shape in the Observations tab?
decisions.rationale: #3 — extend the existing detail-row render pattern decisions already use for markdown bodies.

decisions.question: Which sage roles get their instructions updated for the new observation shape?
decisions.rationale: #36 — complete the set: every code path calling observations-write must match the new strict signature.

decisions.question: Does sage-writer's gap-report semantic (blocking the pipeline when decisions don't cover the design space) change, or only the shape of what it writes?
decisions.rationale: #18 — semantic rework would be speculative abstraction; brief limits change to packaging.

decisions.question: What guidance do the updated role instructions give on observation title content (length, style)?
decisions.rationale: #22 — named reader (overseer curating writs) shapes an explicit title contract.

decisions.question: What is the new engine's designId?
decisions.rationale: #32 — name pressure-tests cleanly across log lines, TS identifier, and rig-template listings.

decisions.question: Is the new engine clockwork or quick (anima-backed)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: What preconditions does the new engine validate before lifting observations?
decisions.rationale: #2 — fail loud on rig-contract precondition violations, matching sibling engines.

decisions.question: What does the engine yield on success?
decisions.rationale: #3 — extend the spec-publish precedent of yielding created-writ identifiers.

decisions.question: How does the engine handle a failure partway through lifting multiple observations (e.g. the third clerk.post() throws)?
decisions.rationale: #2 — fail loud; orphaned-draft recovery beats silent aggregation or speculative rollback.

decisions.question: How does the observation writ inherit its codex — explicit `codex: plan.codex` or rely on Clerk's parentId-inheritance?
decisions.rationale: #3 — extend the spec-publish precedent of passing codex explicitly for diagnostic self-containment.

decisions.question: What does the engine do if it encounters a plan with the legacy `observations: string` shape instead of the new array shape?
decisions.rationale: #11 — skip-when-unset over framework defaults; the new shape opt-in is legitimate framework silence.

decisions.question: What does the engine do when observations is an empty array or undefined?
decisions.rationale: #11 — skip-when-unset; empty observations is a legitimate state, not a pipeline failure.

decisions.question: Which rig templates get the new engine — plan-and-ship only, or all three including the legacy two-phase/three-phase templates?
decisions.rationale: #36 — complete the set: observation-lift is a property of the planning pipeline, not one rig.

decisions.question: Where in the plan-and-ship rig does the observation-lift engine sit?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Where in the two-phase and three-phase rigs does the observation-lift engine sit?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Does the engine patch the plandoc with any tracking field (e.g. `observationWritIds`) after lifting?
decisions.rationale: #19 — don't persist derived state; parentId already makes the relationship queryable.

decisions.question: Does the engine only count Observation records that have non-empty title AND non-empty body, or does it attempt to lift every record in the array regardless?
decisions.rationale: #15 — hold the cross-component contract line; tool boundary is the single validation point.

decisions.question: Does the rig-template entry for observation-lift use `when:` guards or run unconditionally?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Which documentation files get updated as part of this commission?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks cleanly; options don't offer deletion, and both docs plausibly serve distinct readers.

decisions.question: What exactly changes in Astrolabe's `supportKit.writTypes` array?
decisions.rationale: #1 fires cleanly: removal beats keeping unnecessary entries; the alternatives are out-of-scope or churn.

decisions.question: How is the Spider vs Astrolabe `mandate` kit-mapping collision resolved so the winner is deterministic and a Spider-only guild still dispatches mandates to `default` with no additional configuration?
decisions.rationale: #1 fires cleanly: dissolve the collision at the contribution layer by removal rather than introducing precedence machinery (priority fields, last-wins policy flips, smuggled requires-semantics).

decisions.question: Assuming Option A in D2, what is the precise fallback rule in `lookup()`?
decisions.rationale: #2 fires cleanly: narrow the fallback to the single hardcoded builtin rather than resurrecting a broad silent fallback or adding configuration the framework should not own (#11).

decisions.question: What is done about `packages/plugins/oculus/src/oculus.test.ts:316-318` (URL-parsing test that uses the literal string `'brief'` in a query string)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: What happens to English-prose 'brief writ' phrasing in code comments, docstrings, and narrative documentation?
decisions.rationale: #38 (repair drift at the code-change site) and the brief's explicit scope carve-out conflict with a #1 read of full cleanup; resolved toward tactical so co-located comments don't disagree with the diff while respecting the non-goal.

decisions.question: How is the `### Writ Types (contributed to Clerk)` section in `packages/plugins/astrolabe/README.md` (lines 121-125) handled after `brief` is removed?
decisions.rationale: #36 (complete the set — document what's actually contributed) and #38 (delete derived/stale artifacts) conflict; resolved toward retain because piece is a live contribution and the section's job is to document live contributions, not to document brief specifically.

writs.title: Spider's kit-vs-kit mapping precedence is load-order dependent with a silent warning

decisions.question: What enforcement policy should replace the current `console.warn` + first-registered-wins behaviour when two kits contribute a `rigTemplateMappings` entry for the same writ type?
decisions.rationale: #2 (fail-loud over silent fallback) fires cleanly against the existing console.warn + first-wins behaviour.

decisions.question: If D1 chooses `deterministic-winner`, what rule decides which kit wins? (Moot if D1=throw; included so the patron can flip D1 without reopening this decision.)
decisions.rationale: #2 (fail-loud) selects the rule that preserves throw for the genuinely-ambiguous case rather than papering over it with alphabetical.

decisions.question: Should the new precedence rule apply framework-wide (Spider `rigTemplateMappings` + Clerk `writTypes` + Spider `blockTypes` + Fabricator engine designs), or only to the brief's literal subject (`rigTemplateMappings`)?
decisions.rationale: #36 (complete the set) — fixing one merge site while leaving sibling registries on inconsistent silent policies is a half-surface; ship the policy across siblings.

decisions.question: Does Spider continue to contribute the plugin-default `rigTemplateMappings: { mandate: 'default' }` kit entry, or does it move to guild-config convention?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: What name should the extracted constant take?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer; the name reads sensibly across declaration and fallback sites.

decisions.question: Should the constant be exported from the package (re-exported via `index.ts`)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer; the brief explicitly scopes this as an exported constant.

decisions.question: Should `BUILTIN_TYPES` remain a `Set` built from the constant, or be replaced with the constant directly?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks cleanly — confirming the primer; removing the Set would be a second refactor outside the brief's narrow scope.

decisions.question: Should the file header docstring at `clerk.ts:11` (`built-in type ('mandate')`) be updated to reference the constant symbolically?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer; narration-style JSDoc is normal and a rename pass would sweep it coherently.

decisions.question: Should the new constant carry a JSDoc matching the `CASCADE_PARENT_TERMINATION_RESOLUTION` precedent (explaining it as a single source of truth)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer; matching the sibling's single-source-of-truth JSDoc is a local convention call.

writs.title: `PlanDoc.generatedWritId` is documented as legacy but still on the type

decisions.question: Should `generatedWritId` (both the typed field and the plan-detail renderer) be removed entirely, or kept to preserve display of historic plandocs that may still carry the field?
decisions.rationale: Principle #1 fires cleanly: when removal and deprecation both work, remove — the field has no writer and the index signature already preserves historic deserialization.

decisions.question: After the 'Mandate Writ' renderer is removed, how should the coupled regression-guard test assertion (currently requiring `>= 2` canonical `/pages/writs/?writ=` anchors) be updated?
decisions.rationale: Principle #2 fires cleanly: fail-loud over silent-fallback — `=== 1` pins the invariant and catches re-introduction of a second renderer, where `>= 1` would silently absorb it.

decisions.question: Should the `[key: string]: unknown` index signature on `PlanDoc` (types.ts line 15) be kept, given that one of its load-bearing functions has been to tolerate legacy fields like `generatedWritId`?
decisions.rationale: Principle #2 (fail-loud) speaks against silent absorption of unknown fields, but conflicts with trusting the primer's framing that the signature is a load-bearing escape hatch serving historic fields beyond `generatedWritId`; resolved toward scope discipline — a broader index-signature removal is a separate commission.

decisions.question: Should the `spec-publish`-era legacy doc comment (currently explaining why the field exists) be deleted along with the field, or rewritten into a historical note somewhere?
decisions.rationale: Principle #38 fires cleanly: when the subject is removed, a comment about it is a drifting/derived artifact — delete rather than repair into a `HISTORY` note that would itself go stale.

writs.title: `docs/architecture/apparatus/astrolabe.md` carries a stale `refines` link diagram for a mandate that no longer gets posted

decisions.question: Should the `## Writ Linking` section be deleted outright, or rewritten to describe the parent/child relationship observation-lift now produces?
decisions.rationale: #38 fires cleanly — the section duplicates content already carried by the rig narration and engine-table entries; delete beats repair for a duplicated artifact with no named external reader.

writs.title: `oculus.test.ts` URL-parsing test uses retired writ-type literal `'brief'`

decisions.question: What placeholder replaces the literal `'brief'` at `packages/plugins/oculus/src/oculus.test.ts:316-317`?
decisions.rationale: No legacy in new stuff (#10) — opaque placeholder avoids reintroducing retired vocabulary, and aligning with the parent commission's selection keeps merge-order independence.

decisions.question: What does the implementer do if the parent commission (`w-mobzggpr-d37a4769915f`) has already applied the rename to `'bug'` before this commission runs?
decisions.rationale: Prefer delete to repair when the thing is derived/already-resolved (#38) — the target state is already achieved, so close cleanly rather than fabricate a redundant edit.

decisions.question: Does this commission extend beyond the single literal at lines 316-317 to audit other stale test-literal vocabulary in the Oculus package (or more broadly)?
decisions.rationale: MVP is the thinnest slice that answers the named question (#23) — the brief names one file and one literal; broader vocabulary audits are a separate concern and the parent already covers the repo-wide sweep.

decisions.question: Do the Reckoner and the Lattice ship as two packages or one combined package?
decisions.rationale: Apparatus owns its own contribution contract (#9) — Lattice is substrate for multiple future emitters, Reckoner is one.

decisions.question: When the Reckoner observes a writ entering `stuck`, should it emit on every stuck transition or only when the stuck is terminal-for-the-patron (retryable=false OR retry-cap-exhausted)?
decisions.rationale: Fix the source, not the consumer (#31) — clockworks-retry already handles transient stucks upstream; downstream compensation would be duplicative.

decisions.question: How should `pulse.triggerType` values be namespaced?
decisions.rationale: First live writer of extension slot sets precedent (#13) — align with Clerk's existing {pluginId}.{kebab-suffix} grammar.

decisions.question: What should `pulse.linkUrl` contain for writ-scoped pulses in MVP?
decisions.rationale: MVP thinness (#23) vs fail-loud (#2) conflict; resolved toward thin slice since a required webBaseUrl is scope creep and a local URL is broken for remote patrons.

decisions.question: What is the shape of the kit contribution type that channels register through?
decisions.rationale: Apparatus owns its own contribution contract (#9) — channel kits define their own per-guild instance config via factories.

decisions.question: Where does the Discord webhook channel live — bundled in the Lattice package or as a standalone channel kit?
decisions.rationale: Don't bake specifics into the generic layer (#6) — Discord-specific deps don't belong in the substrate.

decisions.question: How should the Reckoner determine 'queue drained'?
decisions.rationale: Hold hard lines on cross-component contracts (#15) — mirroring Spider's trySpawn filter would bind Reckoner to Spider internals.

decisions.question: What does the Lattice do when a channel's `send()` fails (network error, webhook 5xx, non-2xx)?
decisions.rationale: Collapse pipeline before adding retry logic (#4) — single pass with structural failed state visible in inbox.

decisions.question: Which CDC phase should the Reckoner's writ observer run in?
decisions.rationale: Hold hard lines on cross-component contracts (#15) — a pulse is layered observation, not a data cascade; writ liveness must not depend on Lattice health.

decisions.question: How does the Lattice dispatch a pulse to channels — synchronously inside `emit()` or asynchronously via a CDC watcher on the pulses book?
decisions.rationale: Single-pass pipeline (#4) and structural state (#20) conflict; resolved toward structural/crash-safe since CDC queue is not retry logic but durable delivery state.

decisions.question: How should the Discord channel render a pulse in the webhook payload?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Does the Reckoner need guild configuration?
decisions.rationale: Earn new structure from a second consumer (#18) — no knob has a named reader in MVP.

decisions.question: What is the shape of the Lattice's guild configuration and how are channel secrets referenced?
decisions.rationale: Object-shaped config boundaries (#5) — array of typed objects supports multiple instances per channel type and defers per-kit shape to the kit.

decisions.question: What prefix do pulse IDs use?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer's alignment with framework convention.

decisions.question: Does the Lattice ship an Oculus page for pulses in MVP?
decisions.rationale: MVP is the thinnest slice that answers the named question (#23) — brief scoped response surface to CLI + Discord.

decisions.question: Does the Reckoner declare `clockworks-retry` as a hard `requires` or a soft `recommends`?
decisions.rationale: Skip-when-unset over framework defaults for taste (#11) — Reckoner works without the retry apparatus by treating every stuck as terminal.

decisions.question: What tool names expose the CLI inbox, and what flags does `pulse-list` take?
decisions.rationale: Iterate API surface to user ergonomics (#7) — two-tool+flag produces the prescribed CLI command forms.

decisions.question: What is the default time window for `pulse list`?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer's adherence to the brief's prescription.

decisions.question: What is the default sort order for `pulse list`?
decisions.rationale: Iterate API surface to user ergonomics (#7) — patron wants most recent alerts on top.

decisions.question: How does `--live` determine whether a pulse's referent condition still holds?
decisions.rationale: Don't persist derived state (#19) — walk the writ graph on read rather than storing a live-flag; drain exclusion is the honest reading for transient-past events.

decisions.question: What value does `pulse.source` carry and who sets it?
decisions.rationale: The extension point is the act of setting the value (#14) — emitters are the authority on their own identity.

decisions.question: What format is `pulse.summary` — plain text, markdown, or structured?
decisions.rationale: Route new distinctions through existing fields (#17) — structured data already lives in context; duplicating as typed summary fields blurs the split.

decisions.question: Should the Reckoner emit for every writ that enters stuck/failed, or only for commission-root writs (parentId absent)?
decisions.rationale: Name the reader and decision (#22) — patron reads commission-level signals; cascaded N-pulse noise serves no reader.

decisions.question: What is the exhaustive set of MVP trigger types the Reckoner emits?
decisions.rationale: Earn new structure from a second consumer (#18) — brief prescribes this set; cancellation is patron-initiated and has no surprise reader.

decisions.question: Should the Lattice and Reckoner be added to the default guild plugin set in `nsg init`?
decisions.rationale: Earn new structure from a second consumer (#18) — prove the apparatus in real guilds before defaulting.

decisions.question: What permission level do `pulse-list` and `pulse-show` declare?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer's alignment with clerk:read / ratchet:read convention.

decisions.question: On Reckoner startup, should it scan for pre-existing stuck/failed writs and emit pulses for them?
decisions.rationale: Don't persist derived state (#19) — the mark-on-writ alternative reintroduces shadow state; pulses record transitions, not states.

decisions.question: What does the tool handler for `pulse-list` / `pulse-show` return — raw structured data or rendered text?
decisions.rationale: Hold hard lines on cross-component contracts (#15) — rendering is presentation and does not belong inside the tool/API handler.

decisions.question: What methods does the `LatticeApi` (provides) expose?
decisions.rationale: Complete the set — ship sibling operations together (#36) — emit plus list/show/count/resolveId is the coherent read+write surface.

decisions.question: What goes into `pulse.context` per trigger type?
decisions.rationale: Scaffold the slot when future content is known-coming (#37) — documented per-type context shape unlocks Discord embed fields and future consumers.

decisions.question: What pagination defaults does `pulse-list` use?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer's alignment with peer list-tool convention.

writs.title: Writs Generated from Observation Must depend on the originating writ

decisions.question: Which link kind should the observation-lift engine use for the dependency edge?
decisions.rationale: #9 apparatus owns its own contribution contract — Spider already owns the precedence-gate kind; inventing an astrolabe-scoped parallel would teach the framework a concept it doesn't need (#8).

decisions.question: Which direction should the `spider.follows` edge run — new draft writ → brief, or brief → new draft writ?
decisions.rationale: #15 hard line on cross-component contract — the kind's registered semantic fixes the direction; reversing would silently break the gate.

decisions.question: What free-text label should accompany the `spider.follows` kind on the new link?
decisions.rationale: #13 first live writer sets precedent — unanimous existing convention makes this the de-facto canonical label for `spider.follows`.

decisions.question: How should the post + link pair be sequenced within the engine's loop?
decisions.rationale: #4 collapse to a single pass — per-record keeps each observation's post+link atomic; two-pass invites a split-state failure mode where posts succeed but links don't.

decisions.question: How should the engine behave when `clerk.link` throws (e.g. the `spider.follows` kind is not registered because Spider is not installed, or an internal Clerk error)?
decisions.rationale: #2 fail loud — a missing `spider.follows` kind is misconfiguration, not a legitimate degraded state; silent fallback would hide the drift this anima needs to see.

decisions.question: Should astrolabe's plugin-level dependency on `spider` be promoted from `recommends` to `requires` as part of this change?
decisions.rationale: #23 thinnest-slice-that-is-complete vs #31/#38 fix-drift-at-the-source; resolved toward scope containment because the declaration drift predates this brief and obs-1 routes it through the normal follow-up pipeline without being dropped.

decisions.question: Should the engine's `yields` shape change to expose the created link ids (or other link metadata) alongside `writIds`?
decisions.rationale: #18 earn new structure from a second consumer — no downstream yield consumer exists; link ids are recoverable via `clerk.links(writId)` when a caller appears.

decisions.question: Should the engine emit a link for each observation unconditionally, or only when the observation count exceeds some threshold / some future per-observation flag opts out?
decisions.rationale: #18 earn new structure from a second consumer — the brief frames the dependency as categorical; a per-observation flag is a speculative slot nothing asks for.

decisions.question: How much of the engine/apparatus documentation should move alongside this change?
decisions.rationale: #38 prefer fixing drift over letting it grow — all three surfaces carry the same now-inaccurate load-bearing claim; updating them together keeps the contract, README, and architecture doc in lockstep.

writs.title: Writ page in Oculus should show all descendants

decisions.question: How does the writs page fetch a deep-descendant hierarchy — client-side recursion with many list calls, or one server-side tree call?
decisions.rationale: #4 — collapse N client calls into a single server pass; ratchet already set this as the tree-fetch pipeline.

decisions.question: What is the shape of the server-side tree response?
decisions.rationale: #13 — ClickTree set the precedent for tree-response shape; writ-side should match.

decisions.question: Does the `writ-tree` tool accept the same params as `click-tree` (`rootId`, `phase` filter, `depth`, `format`)?
decisions.rationale: #13 — click-tree's param surface is the precedent for tree-tool params across apparatus.

decisions.question: How does the tree endpoint honor phase/type filtering — prune (drop non-matching nodes and their subtrees) or highlight (keep the scaffold, flag matches)?
decisions.rationale: #13 — ratchet's tree() already established prune semantics; writ-list already drops non-matching rows.

decisions.question: How is depth visualized in the table — keep the flat-table shape with progressive indent, or switch to a nested `<ul>/<li>` tree like ratchet's clicks page?
decisions.rationale: #28 — drill-down table with inspect is Sean's default UI pattern; keep the columns, add depth indent.

decisions.question: What is the maximum indent depth (number of visible levels beneath a root) before the '…' overflow indicator takes over?
decisions.rationale: #18 — viewport-adaptive and user-configurable are speculative; the brief named 8.

decisions.question: What does the overflow indicator row look like when a subtree exceeds the indent cap, and what does clicking it do?
decisions.rationale: #28 — drill-down via table row click matches the established pattern; badge/inert ellipsis dead-end the operator.

decisions.question: Does each row get a per-node expand/collapse toggle, or is hierarchy always fully expanded client-side (subject only to the depth cap)?
decisions.rationale: #13 — ratchet's per-node toggle is the precedent for deep-tree navigation.

decisions.question: Is the default expand state `all expanded` or `roots only`?
decisions.rationale: #18 — remember-last is speculative; all-expanded matches both ratchet and current writs behavior.

decisions.question: Sort-column behavior for deep descendants: do the root-level column sorts (Phase/Title/Type/ID/Created) also apply inside each subtree, or do descendants remain in a stable per-parent order?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: How does the title-search text filter behave in a deep-descendant view — match any row and drop the rest, keep ancestor context, or match roots only?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Is the global 'Children' toggle button retained after per-node toggles ship?
decisions.rationale: #1 (prefer removal) vs #23 (thinnest slice); resolved toward retention because the global collapse-all is not subsumed by per-node toggles.

decisions.question: Where does the server-side tree walker live — a new `clerk.tree()` ClerkApi method or a utility function on the `writ-tree` tool handler?
decisions.rationale: #13 — ratchet's RatchetApi.tree() set the precedent; keep the walker reusable on the apparatus API.

decisions.question: Does the new `writ-tree` tool include `callableBy: ['patron']` (exposed via Oculus REST), or is it unrestricted?
decisions.rationale: #11 — skip-when-unset; matches click-tree and every other read-only Clerk tool.

decisions.question: Does the writs page fetch the entire tree on page load (subject to depth cap) or lazy-load descendants on expand?
decisions.rationale: #4 — single bounded RPC beats lazy-expand retries; caps keep it safe at scale.

decisions.question: Does the in-memory tree response drive pagination, or do we keep the separate `writ-list` root-paginated call and then fetch deep subtrees per batch?
decisions.rationale: #3 — extend writ-tree's surface with rootLimit/rootOffset rather than routing around it with a second call.

decisions.question: What is the new default root page size (current `LIMIT`)?
decisions.rationale: #18 — brief named 100; 200 is speculative stretch with no named consumer.

decisions.question: How are indent levels rendered in CSS — inline `padding-left` on each `<td>`, or a CSS variable driven by a `data-depth` attribute?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: What happens to the `isChild` boolean in `sortedFilteredWrits` — replace with `depth: number`, keep both, or reshape to a tree structure?
decisions.rationale: #1 — remove isChild rather than keep both; depth generalizes the boolean cleanly.

decisions.question: Does the detail view's children table also recurse to show descendants, or stay at direct-children?
decisions.rationale: #23 — thinnest slice that answers the brief's scoped question; detail view is the drill-down target.

decisions.question: Does `LIMIT` become configurable (per-user preference or query param) or remain a code constant?
decisions.rationale: #18 — no second consumer has asked for configurability; URL param or settings UI is speculative.

writs.title: Enable goal editing on live clicks via `nsg click amend`

decisions.question: What name should the history field use on the click record: `goalHistory` (camelCase) or `goal_history` (snake_case, as sketched in the brief)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: What should each goalHistory entry contain, and what should its fields be named?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Should goalHistory entries be appended (oldest-first) or prepended (newest-first)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: How should the no-op amend (identical goal text) be detected — strict string equality, trimmed equality, or something else?
decisions.rationale: Fail-loud over silent fallback (#2) — the substrate should not silently normalize whitespace.

decisions.question: Should the amend implementation wrap the read-modify-write in a stacks.transaction() to keep concurrent amends safe, or follow the existing conclude/drop pattern of a non-transactional read-then-patch?
decisions.rationale: Fail-loud (#2) — silent loss of a history entry under concurrency is the opposite of the audit guarantee the field exists to provide.

decisions.question: Should amend's API method and tool accept an optional sessionId the same way create/conclude/drop do, and what should the param be named?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: How should amend reject a non-live click — with a reusable validateTransition-style error, or with a bespoke message that names the current status?
decisions.rationale: Word-evokes-wrong-image (#33) — 'transition' misrepresents amend, which is not a status transition.

decisions.question: Should amend also reject empty / whitespace-only goal text, matching the conclude/drop pattern that rejects empty `conclusion` (`.trim() === ''`)?
decisions.rationale: Fail-loud (#2) — amend is a mutating write of a user-facing string; silently accepting empty would corrupt every surface that displays the click.

decisions.question: Should the `goalHistory` field be a typed named field on ClickDoc, or rely on the index signature `[key: string]: unknown`?
decisions.rationale: Fail-loud (#2) — the index signature is tolerance, not a license to skip declarations; typed field catches shape drift at compile time.

decisions.question: What should the new API method be named — `amend(id, params)` or `amendGoal(id, params)` or something else?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: What to do with the existing `describe('goal immutability', ...)` block at ratchet.test.ts:391-401 whose premise is overturned by this change?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Does click-show need explicit code to expose goalHistory, or does the existing `{ ...click, links, parent, children }` spread at tools/click-show.ts:40 handle it automatically?
decisions.rationale: Earn new structure from a second consumer (#18) — and don't persist/expose derived state (#19); a computed length field has no named reader yet.

decisions.question: Should `click-show`'s markdown-rendering path (`renderMarkdown` at ratchet.ts:151-177) be updated to surface a hint about goalHistory presence in human-readable output? The brief says 'human output should at minimum indicate that prior versions exist and how to see them.'
decisions.rationale: MVP is the thinnest slice that answers the named question (#23) — the brief explicitly fences tree/extract markdown out of scope, and the JSON dump already meets the stated minimum.

decisions.question: Which docs/README edits are load-bearing for this commission, and which are pre-existing drift that should stay out of scope?
decisions.rationale: Complete the set (#36) — leaving the spec claiming 'goal immutable' while the code amends is an incoherent surface; pre-existing unrelated drift is a separate commission.

decisions.question: Should the create-side `goal` field also have empty-string validation added while we are touching goal mutation semantics (since create allows `{ goal: '' }` today and amend rejects empty)?
decisions.rationale: Earn new structure from a second consumer (#18) and MVP is the thinnest slice (#23) — widening to create-validation is scope creep the brief does not prescribe.

decisions.question: Should the amend method return the full updated ClickDoc (including new goalHistory) or just the fields that changed?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: What permission level does the click-amend tool declare?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

writs.title: Extend detail-view children table to show full descendant tree

decisions.question: Where does the detail view get the deep descendant tree — a new call to the parent brief's writ-tree endpoint with rootId, or keep the existing writ-list?parentId fan-out and recurse client-side?
decisions.rationale: #3 fires cleanly — writ-tree with rootId is the existing API extension point; client-side recursion is routing around it.

decisions.question: Does the detail view still call writ-show (for parent context, links, summary) alongside writ-tree, or does writ-tree alone drive the detail render?
decisions.rationale: #15 fires against merging writ-tree's deep-descendants output into writ-show's contract — hold the hard line between the two tools' responsibilities.

decisions.question: What depth cap does the detail view use for its subtree render?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks to the specific cap value — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: What does the detail-view overflow row do when clicked?
decisions.rationale: #26 fires — the detail-view drill-in is the existing surface; inline-expand and non-interactive both invent new affordances without cause.

decisions.question: Does the detail view get per-node expand/collapse toggles, or does it always render the subtree fully expanded (subject to the cap)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks to toggle vs always-expanded — no-toggle-roots-only is eliminated on its own terms; confirming the primer between the remaining two.

decisions.question: Is the detail view's collapsed-set shared with the main list's collapsed-set, or scoped to the detail view and reset on each open?
decisions.rationale: #18 fires — persistence and cross-view sharing both add structure without a named second consumer; reset is the earn-it-later default.

decisions.question: How is the depth-aware rendering logic organized between the main list and the detail view — extract a shared pure helper, or duplicate the depth/overflow/toggle logic inline in renderDetail()?
decisions.rationale: #15 fires — generalizing sortedFilteredWrits would merge sort/search concerns across the main-list/detail-view contract; a narrower shared helper keeps the boundary clean.

decisions.question: Does the shallow children-summary badge strip (above the children table) change — extend to a deep rollup, keep shallow, or remove?
decisions.rationale: #23 fires — this brief's named question is deep-descendants visibility, not summary-strip shape; obs-2 is explicitly separate scope.

decisions.question: Do the per-row Actions buttons (row-publish, row-cancel) and the row-click-to-open-detail handler apply to deep-descendant rows, or only to direct children?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks to action-column scope — confirming the primer's main-list-parity call.

decisions.question: Column set of the detail-view children table — stays at the current narrow set (Phase | Title | Type | ID | Actions, no Created), or align with the main list (add Created)?
decisions.rationale: #23 fires — the brief did not name a column-set question; status quo is the thinnest slice.

decisions.question: Does the detail view gain a global Children toggle button (the main list's 'just show roots' shortcut), or stay without one?
decisions.rationale: #18 fires — a global toggle is new structure with no named consumer; per-node toggles already cover targeted collapse.

decisions.question: When the detail view's writ has no descendants at all (truly childless), does the Children section render an empty table, an empty-state message, or hide entirely as it does today?
decisions.rationale: #23 fires — empty-state UX is not the named question; status quo keeps scope disciplined.

writs.title: Patron Anima Never Runs - But It Should Review EVERY Decision

decisions.question: Fix shape: delete reviewableDecisions() and review every decision, or invert the filter so 'reviewable = every decision' in attended mode?
decisions.rationale: #1 fires cleanly — removal over deprecation; 'invert' keeps a hook for a speculative future mixed-mode.

decisions.question: How should anima abstentions surface to the patron after the fix? (decision-review interaction)
decisions.rationale: #3 fires cleanly — route through the existing `selected === undefined` contract instead of inventing a parallel 'abstain' verdict (#18 also: no second consumer for the new type).

decisions.question: When clearing an abstained decision's `selected`, should patron-anima also clear `Decision.patronOverride` (defensive reset) or leave it alone?
decisions.rationale: #2 (fail-loud: prevent a silent half-state where stale patronOverride hides an abstained decision) conflicts with #18 (don't defend against hypothetical future rig orderings); resolved toward #2 because the cost is a single delete.

decisions.question: What to do with the broken-contract test 'no-ops when every decision is already pre-decided by the primer' (patron-anima.test.ts:290-306)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — delete-plus-add and rewrite are operationally equivalent; confirming the primer.

decisions.question: What to do with the second broken-contract test 'only reviewable decisions are prompted; pre-decided ones are left alone' (patron-anima.test.ts:800-838)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks between delete and rewrite (keep is ruled out by #18 as defending an unreachable path); confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Where should the 'primer-attended + patron-anima agree on reviewable' contract assertion live?
decisions.rationale: #18 fires — 'both' adds a redundant text-grep assertion with no second consumer, and locking editorial wording of sage prose is not a machine contract.

decisions.question: Add a test specifically covering the abstention-surfacing flow (D2's clear-selected behaviour) end-to-end?
decisions.rationale: #18 fires — the handoff/both variants re-test decision-review's already-pinned filter, defending a path no new consumer has earned.

decisions.question: Update patron-anima.ts JSDoc to reflect the new contract?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks directly to JSDoc repair; confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Update reader-analyst.ts JSDoc paragraph (L20-24) that describes the broken reviewable partition?
decisions.rationale: #38 fires — the paragraph duplicates patron-anima's internal behaviour into a sibling engine's JSDoc; delete the duplicate rather than maintain two copies.

decisions.question: Update docs/architecture/apparatus/astrolabe.md L154 parenthetical that paraphrases the bug?
decisions.rationale: #1 fires — the parenthetical only existed to describe the removed code path; remove rather than rewrite a non-load-bearing paraphrase.

decisions.question: Update README.md L144 (engines table description of patron-anima)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks directly to README repair vs skip; confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Should the implementation commission update the c-mo9hnid0 click as part of its workstream, or is this a curator action left to the patron?
decisions.rationale: #9 fires — click curation is Ratchet/curator contribution, not engine work; don't bundle curatorial state-mutation into an implement rig.

decisions.question: Should patron-anima retain a fast-path for the case `plan.decisions` is empty / undefined (no decisions at all)?
decisions.rationale: #11 fires — skip-when-empty is the same shape as skip-when-unset; framework supplies no taste on empty plans, and the brief did not flag empty-plan as a bug to tighten.

decisions.question: When patron-anima clears `selected` for abstained decisions in collect(), should it also clear/reset `Decision.patron` if it was set in a previous run (idempotency)?
decisions.rationale: #18 (no second consumer for retry idempotency) conflicts with #2 (stale audit trail is silently misleading); resolved toward #18 — the re-run path is hypothetical and decision-review ignores patron when selected is undefined.

writs.title: writ-show children summary is only first-generation; consider deep summary

decisions.question: Which of the brief's two suggested paths should this commission take: a `summary.deep` counter on the writ-show response, or a separate `writ-tree` mode that returns just counts?
decisions.rationale: #23 fires cleanly — thinnest slice that answers the named at-a-glance inspection question; writ-tree belongs to its own sibling commission.

decisions.question: How should the subtree-wide counts be exposed in the writ-show response — replace the existing `children.summary` semantics, add a parallel field, or nest `deep` inside summary?
decisions.rationale: #1 fires cleanly — no non-UI consumer to preserve, so remove the shallow semantics rather than carry a parallel field.

decisions.question: Should the descendant-count computation be exposed as a public method on ClerkApi, or live inline inside the writ-show handler?
decisions.rationale: #3 fires cleanly — extend the existing ClerkApi surface rather than route around it with a handler-local helper.

decisions.question: What should the new ClerkApi method be named, and what shape should it return?
decisions.rationale: #32 fires cleanly — 'count descendants by phase' survives the log-line/prose/identifier pressure-test with no ambiguity about root-inclusion or keying.

decisions.question: Should the count include the root writ itself, or strictly its descendants?
decisions.rationale: #15 fires cleanly — ratchet's rootId convention already set the cross-apparatus precedent; don't concatenate a different semantics on the writ side.

decisions.question: Should the same subtree-wide summary treatment be applied to ratchet's click-show in this commission?
decisions.rationale: #23 (narrow MVP, brief names only writ) conflicts with #36 (clicks and writs are structural siblings; the same widening applies identically); resolved toward #36 since drift between parallel apparatuses is the exact thing complete-the-set prevents.

decisions.question: How should the descendant walk be implemented — recursive `find({ where: [['parentId','=',id]] })` per level, or a one-shot fetch of all writs filtered in memory?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Should the descendant walk enforce a depth cap?
decisions.rationale: #18 fires cleanly — no measured problem and no second consumer earns the depth-cap structure.

decisions.question: Should the descendant walk include a runtime cycle guard (e.g. a `visited` Set)?
decisions.rationale: #12 fires cleanly — parentId immutability is a framework guarantee held by the phase machine; a reader-side guard duplicates enforcement for a state that cannot legally exist.

decisions.question: Should the `children.items` list also widen to include descendants, or stay direct-children-only?
decisions.rationale: #23 fires cleanly — the named question is subtree *count* at a glance; widening items is a separate decision that belongs with writ-tree.

decisions.question: Is a writ-tree tool in scope for this commission?
decisions.rationale: #23 fires cleanly — sibling mandate already tracks writ-tree; doubling scope pulls in UI-layer work outside the brief.

decisions.question: How should the Oculus writs page detail view present the widened summary strip?
decisions.rationale: #32 fires cleanly — the existing comment would be actively wrong post-widening (pressure-test failure), and a 'Descendants' label is the user-facing counterpart of the same clarity.

decisions.question: How should the Oculus page-script test `summary badges reflect direct children only` (writs-hierarchy.test.js ~line 741) be updated?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks to test-update mechanics — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: Should performance caching be introduced for the descendant walk?
decisions.rationale: #18 fires cleanly — no measured contention and no second consumer earns a materialized cache slot.

decisions.question: Which clerk docs need updating, and how?
decisions.rationale: #35 fires cleanly — clerk.md is the architecture-layer contract that must track shape; README is the tour layer and shape detail doesn't belong there.

decisions.question: What test coverage should this commission add?
decisions.rationale: #36 fires cleanly — having chosen to include click-show in D6, completing the set means click-show gets its multi-level test alongside clerk's.

decisions.question: How should the writ-show tool's `instructions` string (the description returned by the tool registry) reflect the change?
decisions.rationale: #32 fires cleanly — tool instructions are the anima-facing surface and must survive the 'what does this return?' pressure-test without requiring a doc lookup.
